results.label: DPA — international transfer with no mechanism
missingClauses: GDPR Article 28 mandatory processor obligations (instructions, confidentiality, audit rights, deletion/return)
International transfer mechanism (SCCs / EU-US DPF certification)
Special category data safeguards and DPIA reference
HIPAA Business Associate Agreement terms (if HIPAA applies)
Data Protection Officer / contact point designation
positiveClauses: Data Retention — Clause 4 provides reasonable retention and deletion timeline (30 days post-termination)

issues: Red flags: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism | Does not flag health data as special category requiring additional protection
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism

results.label: DPA — well drafted, minimal issues expected
missingClauses: DPIA and Prior Consultation Assistance (GDPR Art. 28(3)(f))
positiveClauses: Clause 2 — Scope and Instructions: Properly limits processing to documented instructions including transfer restrictions per Art. 28(3)(a)
Clause 3 — Confidentiality: Meets Art. 28(3)(b) requirement for confidentiality commitments
Clause 4 — Security (Article 32): Strong and specific — includes AES-256, TLS 1.3, access controls, and regular testing
Clause 5 — Sub-processors: Well-drafted with prior written authorisation, listed sub-processors, and 30-day advance notice of changes per Art. 28(2)
Clause 6 — Data Subject Rights: Clear 5-business-day response commitment for Art. 15–22 assistance
Clause 7 — Breach Notification: Exceeds GDPR Art. 33(2) requirements with a 24-hour notification window
Clause 9 — Deletion: Clear 30-day deletion/return obligation with written certification upon termination per Art. 28(3)(g)

results.label: DPA — HIPAA context, missing BAA obligations
missingClauses: Individual Rights (Access, Amendment, Accounting of Disclosures)
positiveClauses: Section 1 — Services: clearly identifies the nature of services and categories of PHI processed

issues: Missing flags: workforce training, contingency plan
Red flags: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards

issues: Missing flags: no exceptions, required by law

redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the absence of standard confidentiality exceptions

Does not flag unlimited damages as an aggressive and likely unenforceable provision

results.label: NDA — one-way, protects discloser only
missingClauses: Governing Law and Jurisdiction — no governing law is specified, creating enforceability risk
Term and Termination — no mechanism for either party to terminate the NDA
Remedies and Injunctive Relief — no acknowledgment that breach may cause irreparable harm or entitle equitable relief
Data Protection / Personal Data Handling — if personal data is shared, a DPA or GDPR/CCPA-compliant processing clause is required
Breach Notification — no obligation to notify the Disclosing Party of unauthorized access or disclosure
No Warranty / No License — standard clause disclaiming implied warranties and clarifying no IP license is granted
Non-Solicitation — commonly included in commercial NDAs to prevent solicitation of employees
positiveClauses: Purpose Limitation — Clause 2 appropriately limits use of Confidential Information to evaluating a potential partnership
Time-Bound Obligation — Clause 2 includes a defined 3-year confidentiality period

issues: Missing flags: one-way, required by law, residuals
Red flags: Does not flag that the agreement is titled 'Mutual' but only protects Discloser | Does not flag incomplete exceptions list missing 'independently developed' and 'required by law'
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag that the agreement is titled 'Mutual' but only protects Discloser
Does not flag incomplete exceptions list missing 'independently developed' and 'required by law'
